Reading: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly
Determine central ideas or themes; analyze their development
Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop
Interpret words and phrases as used in a text
Analyze the structure of texts
Assess how point of view or purpose shapes content and style
Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media
Delineate and evaluate the argument in a text
Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes
Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts
Writing: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and evidence
Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey ideas
Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences
Develop and strengthen writing through planning and revising
Use technology to produce and publish writing
Conduct short and sustained research projects
Gather relevant information from multiple sources
Draw evidence from literary or informational texts
Write routinely over extended and shorter time frames

Reading: Cite textual evidence to support analysis
Determine central ideas; provide objective summary
Analyze how text unfolds and ideas are introduced
Determine meaning of words and phrases including figurative language
Analyze how sentences, paragraphs, and sections fit together
Assess point of view and purpose; distinguish from what is claimed
Integrate and evaluate multiple sources of information
Delineate and evaluate the argument including false statements
Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes
Read and comprehend complex texts independently
